# Conflict Analysis Requirements Checklist: UC-24 Warn About Time Conflicts in Candidate Schedules

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, consistency, and measurability of UC-24 conflict-analysis requirements before implementation
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/024-branch-conflict-warnings/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for all four analysis outcomes: conflicts found, conflict-free, incomplete analysis, and unavailable timetable data? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK002 Are the required user-visible details for each detected conflict specified beyond a generic warning label? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec define what must happen when a candidate schedule has only one meeting component? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Are re-analysis requirements defined after the student changes selections or constraints and regenerates schedules? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the definition of a conflict unambiguous about overlap versus back-to-back meetings? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK006 Is the term "conflicting course components" specific enough to determine what identifiers or meeting details must be shown to the student? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK007 Is "incomplete analysis" defined clearly enough to distinguish unknown, TBA, and partial meeting-time cases from confirmed conflicts? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK008 Is "retrying later" defined clearly enough to know whether the requirement is only messaging or also requires a dedicated recovery action? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-006]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are the overlap-only conflict rules consistent between the clarification log, functional requirements, assumptions, and edge cases? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK010 Do the user stories and success criteria use the same state names and meanings for incomplete and unavailable analysis? [Consistency, Spec §User Stories, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK011 Are the dependencies on generated candidate schedules and timetable data consistent between the spec and the plan? [Consistency, Spec §Dependencies, Plan §Summary, Plan §Technical Context]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Can each success criterion be objectively verified without relying on unstated UI behavior? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK013 Are acceptance scenarios defined for both positive and failure-path outcomes rather than only the main success path? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [x] CHK014 Is the requirement to remain traceable to `UC-24` and `UC-24-AS` supported by explicit story-to-artifact references? [Traceability, Spec §FR-009, Spec §User Stories]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are primary, alternate, and exception scenarios all represented in requirements for conflict detection, conflict-free confirmation, incomplete analysis, and unavailable data? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK016 Does the spec define what happens when multiple distinct conflicts exist in the same candidate schedule? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK017 Are requirements clear about whether analysis applies to one selected candidate schedule or multiple schedules at once? [Coverage, Gap]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are touching-but-non-overlapping meetings addressed explicitly in the requirements? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK019 Are unknown, TBA, and partial meeting-time cases addressed consistently as incomplete analysis rather than silent omission or hard failure? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK020 Does the spec define the expected requirement behavior when timetable data becomes unavailable after schedules have already been generated? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Dependencies]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK021 Are performance expectations for conflict-analysis result delivery explicitly defined in the requirements rather than only in the plan? [Gap]
- [x] CHK022 Are accessibility requirements specified for warnings, confirmations, and incomplete/unavailable states? [Gap]
- [x] CHK023 Are privacy requirements defined for schedule-analysis views so only the authenticated student's candidate schedules are inspectable? [Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK024 Are assumptions about authenticated access and pre-generated schedules clearly separated from functional requirements? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK025 Are external dependency expectations for course/timetable data availability documented with enough detail to validate failure scenarios? [Dependency, Spec §Dependencies, Spec §FR-006]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK026 Is the scope intentionally limited to advisory warnings, with no hidden implication of automatic schedule repair or enrollment changes? [Conflict, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK027 Are any remaining open issues from `UC-24.md` either resolved in the spec or intentionally excluded with clear wording? [Ambiguity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §Requirements]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Checklist review completed on 2026-03-11; resulting spec updates added warning-detail specificity, single-schedule scope, retry-guidance wording, and explicit non-functional requirements.
